title: Orthogonal Concept Erasure for Diffusion Models
abstract: Orthogonal Concept Erasure (OCE) is a geometry-driven editing method for text-to-image diffusion concept erasure. It replaces additive parameter updates with layer-wise orthogonal transformations, changing neuron directions while preserving magnitudes and inter-neuron angular geometry. OCE further uses subspace-level erasure with vector-wise preservation for scalable multi-concept erasure, achieving strong erasure-preservation trade-offs and erasing up to 100 concepts in 4.3 seconds.
publication_status: arXiv preprint; TeX style uses ICML 2026 template
